subject: ap-chemistry
title: Chemical Kinetics & Rate Laws
stimulus: The complete photoelectron spectrum of an element is represented above.

parts.prompt:
Identify the element.
A radioactive isotope of the element decays with a half-life of 10. minutes.

alternatives.correct_example: The element is nitrogen, N. 1 point is earned for correctly identifying the element. A radioactive isotope of the element decays with a half-life of 10. minutes.

parts.prompt: Calculate the value of the rate constant, k, for the radioactive decay. Include units with your answer.

scoring_points.description: Calculate the value of the rate constant, k, for the radioactive decay. Include units with your answer.

required_elements: Calculate the value of the rate constant, k, for the radioactive decay. Include units with your answer.
alternatives.correct_example: OR 1 point is earned for the correct answer and a valid method.

parts.prompt:
If 64 atoms of the radioactive isotope are originally present in a sample, what is the expected amount of time
that will pass until only one atom of the isotope remains? Show how you arrived at your answer.

scoring_points.description: the correct answer and a valid method. © 2018 The College Board. Visit the College Board on the Web: www.collegeboard.org

required_elements: the correct answer and a valid method. © 2018 The College Board. Visit the College Board on the Web: www.collegeboard.org
alternatives.correct_example: lives are required. 6 10. min= 60. min OR ln[A]1 ln[A] = kt t= ln(l) ln(64) = 60. min min
